textPlain: The OP says that 8-bit CPUs couldn't handle Pascal well, and that Action! (release in 1983) was the first IDE for 8-bit machines.But Apple Pascal was released for the Apple II in 1979.  Based on UCSD Pascal, the Apple Pascal system was basically an OS that simply was an IDE; and it worked perfectly well on 8-bit hardware.  I had quite a lot of fun with it back in the day.
ActionScript came ~10-15 years later. I would be very surprised if there was any relation.
